patches.summary: Omit Authorization header for MercadoLibre's public endpoints (/classified_locations/*) so the CLI works without a token AND for users with an expired/invalid token.
patches.reason: Discovered during release v0.1.0 smoke test: the generated HTTP client sends Authorization: Bearer <token> on every request when a token is present in config. MercadoLibre rejects expired tokens with HTTP 401 even on endpoints that don't require auth (like /classified_locations/countries). The README documented this endpoint as 'public — no auth required', but the CLI made it unreachable for any user whose 6h token had expired. Patch adds an isPublicPath() check that clears the auth header for known public path prefixes.
patches.validated_outcome: Built local binary, ran `countries list` with (a) no token, (b) expired token, (c) explicitly invalid token via env — all 3 returned HTTP 200 with 24 countries. `catalog search` with valid token still works (regression check passed).

deferred_to_upstream.feature: Per-endpoint auth flag in the Printing Press spec format (e.g. `endpoints.<name>.auth: false` or top-level `public_path_prefixes`), so the generator emits this guard automatically based on the spec instead of requiring a manual patch.
deferred_to_upstream.reason: Spec format currently has only a top-level `auth` block applied to all endpoints. Many APIs (ML, Stripe webhooks, Twitter status, etc.) mix public and authenticated endpoints; the generator should honor that without per-CLI patches.

patches.summary: Add `auth login` command with browser OAuth dance + local callback server.
patches.reason: ML requires per-user OAuth app + manual code copy from redirect URL; the wizard eliminates the 5-step manual flow (visit URL, copy ?code= from redirect, curl /oauth/token, paste, save) and makes the CLI usable for non-technical end users. Saves client_id + client_secret + refresh_token so the transparent-token-refresh patch can keep the session alive without external cron.
patches.validated_outcome: Built binary; `auth login --help` shows full flag set (--port, --site-id) with Spanish prose. `--no-input` correctly errors with 'auth login requires interactive mode'. Listener pre-binds before browser open so port-in-use error fires cleanly. Cross-platform browser open (cmd /c start, open, xdg-open) selected via runtime.GOOS.

deferred_to_upstream.feature: Native OAuth Authorization Code flow in Printing Press generator (spec-level `auth.oauth2.flow: authorization_code` with `callback_port` and per-site `domain_map`).
deferred_to_upstream.reason: Many APIs (ML, Slack, GitHub Apps, Notion) need the local-callback dance; today the generator only knows Client Credentials and Bearer. A generator-native flow would eliminate per-CLI wizards and standardize UX across the catalog.

patches.summary: Auto-refresh access token before requests when <30min to expiry; mutex-protected to collapse parallel refreshes.
patches.reason: ML tokens live 6h; without transparent refresh users hit 401s and need external cron jobs (schtasks on Windows, crontab on Linux). Logic lives in client.maybeRefresh() called from authHeader(); uses stdlib net/http (NOT the surf client) to avoid recursion through Client.do. On failure logs a stderr warning and falls through to the current token rather than blocking the user's command.
patches.validated_outcome: Built binary; `auth status` still passes with existing tokens. Refresh only triggers when full OAuth trio is present (refresh_token + client_id + client_secret), so env-token-only users are untouched. Logs '[ml-cli] token refreshed (valid until HH:MM:SS)' to stderr on success.

deferred_to_upstream.feature: Generator-native refresh-token rotation hook (`auth.oauth2.refresh_token: true` triggers transparent refresh + persistence).
deferred_to_upstream.reason: Every Authorization Code OAuth API needs this; today every CLI built from the catalog re-invents it. Pairing this with the OAuth flow upstream patch would make ML-style auth work out of the box.

patches.summary: items search subcommand: catalog→/products/{id}/items workflow with client-side filter+sort and noise-domain pre-filter.
patches.reason: ML deprecated the public /sites/{id}/search endpoint in 2024 (HTTP 403 even with valid token). Real listings with price are only reachable via catalog→/products/{id}/items. This workflow papers over that API limitation. Concurrent fan-out (semaphore of 5), client-side filter parser (price=MIN-MAX, condition, shipping_cost=free, seller, currency), sort (price_asc/desc, sold_desc, relevance), and noise-domain skip (_SCREENS, _CASES, _ACCESSORIES, etc.) to avoid returning phone cases when the user searched for phones. Breaking change: top-level `items <id>` becomes `items get <id>`.
patches.validated_outcome: End-to-end smoke test 2026-05-25: `items search --q "motorola edge 60" --site-id MLA --sort price_asc --filter price=0-1000000 --limit 5 --json` returned 5 real listings (MLA3133009084 et al, prices 414729-519800 ARS, all under 1M filter), total_before_filter=81. Real permalinks (https://articulo.mercadolibre.com.ar/MLA-...). Noise domains (CELLPHONE_SCREENS, CASES) correctly skipped pre-fanout. `items get <id>` preserves old detail behavior.

deferred_to_upstream.feature: Workflow-as-codegen primitive in Printing Press spec format (`workflows.<name>.steps: [catalog_search, fanout, filter, sort, limit]`).
deferred_to_upstream.reason: Many APIs require composing 2-3 endpoints because the natural query endpoint is unavailable, rate-limited, or returns less detail than the fanout path. A spec-level workflow primitive would replace ad-hoc Go orchestration files like this one with generated code that respects pagination, rate limits, and concurrency caps uniformly.
